page: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs based on the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz across 48 physical cores. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For hardware acceleration, the instance bundles four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with a combined VRAM of 89 GB. Local storage consists of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. The instance achieves top-tier results for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models, with the 70B model processing prompts at 397.89 tokens per second and generating text at 10.7 tokens per second. Passmark CPU encryption tests also rank in the top tier. While single-core CPU performance in stress-ng is poor, multi-core CPU performance and software compilation benchmarks remain average. Graphics-related tasks, such as Geekbench ray tracing and image processing, show strong performance.

Given the high density of GPU resources and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, the g5.24xlarge offers a specialized hardware profile optimized for parallel computing. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps network bandwidth supports data-intensive pipelines without external storage bottlenecks. This configuration is highly suited for workloads such as LLM inference, machine learning model training, ray tracing, image processing, and high-throughput database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance designed for compute-intensive and graphics-heavy workloads. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features 96 dedicated vCPUs via an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance integrates four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with a total of 89 GB of VRAM, delivering top-tier performance in large language model inference and strong results in ray tracing and image processing. While single-core CPU performance is limited, its multi-core capabilities and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth provide robust throughput. This server is optimized for machine learning inference, data science, and parallel rendering applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, and four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs for high-performance LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.24xlarge features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, four NVIDIA A10G GPUs, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring four NVIDIA Ampere accelerators and local NVMe storage for demanding machine learning and rendering workloads.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM
96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs operating at 3.3 GHz
384 GB DDR4 RAM and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference and cryptographic tasks
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth via Nitro hypervisor
Suited for machine learning, ray tracing, and data-intensive pipelines
